items.title: Our Plan for a Sustainably Open Social Network
items.summary: We've been exploring avenues of monetization other than traditional advertising, and have raised a seed round to support our mission and growth.

items.title: Purchase and Manage Domains Directly Through Bluesky
items.summary: We’re excited to announce a new feature that allows users to seamlessly purchase and manage domains directly through Bluesky.

items.title: Moderation in a Public Commons
items.summary: In this post, we share why we believe a public commons is important for social media, as well as some proposals for moderation and safety tooling.

items.title: What is Bluesky?
items.summary: On Bluesky, users own their data, developers will never get locked out of the ecosystems they help build, and creators own their relationships with their audience.

items.title: Private Beta Update & Roadmap
items.summary: Our priorities continue to be a focus on moderation and curation to ensure a safe and pleasant environment for users, and protocol work to launch federation.

items.title: Bluesky User FAQ
items.summary: Welcome to the Bluesky app! This is a user guide that answers some common questions.

items.title: Federation Architecture Overview
items.summary: Soon, we’re launching a sandbox environment for federation. In advance, we want to share some technical details about our design decisions.

items.title: How to verify your Bluesky account
items.summary: Here's how to verify your Bluesky account by setting your website as your username.

items.title: The AT Protocol Developer Ecosystem
items.summary: The AT Protocol's developer ecosystem is growing rapidly. Today we’re shipping app passwords, a short-term solution for authentication that will let users experiment with new clients without having to fully trust them.

items.title: Composable Moderation
items.summary: A customizable, composable approach to moderation that prioritizes safety and gives users and developers more control.

items.title: Algorithmic choice
items.summary: Your attention is an invaluable resource. We want a future where you control what you see on social media.

items.title: Domain Names as Handles in Bluesky
items.summary: Today we got our first custom domain name handle registered on Bluesky. Domain name handles are a way for us to improve the state of trust and control users have over their social identities online.

items.title: Bluesky Private Beta Update
items.summary: We’ve started inviting people from our waitlist to test out a beta app. This app will be a reference client for the AT Protocol, and a landing place for users to enter the ecosystem.

items.title: The AT Protocol
items.summary: In the spring, we released “ADX,” the very first iteration of the protocol. Over the summer we improved ADX’s design, and today we’re sharing a preview of what’s to come.

items.title: Working In Public
items.summary: It’s been four months since the Bluesky company started hiring a team. Now that we have a few people on board, we’re starting to work in public. Today we’re releasing ADX, the “Authenticated Data Experiment”.

items.title: A Self-Authenticating Social Protocol
items.summary: Bluesky’s mission is to drive the evolution from platforms to protocols. The conceptual framework we've adopted for meeting this objective is the "self-authenticating protocol."

items.title: The Initial Bluesky Team
items.summary: Announcing the initial members of the Bluesky team.

items.title: How It Started: Three Phases
items.summary: Bluesky was announced in 2019 but the legal entity itself was only recently set up. In the meantime, the bluesky community took shape and has taken on a life of its own.

items.title: Announcing Bluesky PBLLC
items.summary: In 2019, Twitter announced bluesky, a project it would fund to create an open and decentralized standard for social media. Since then...
